Resource title: Excerpts from "RuVector: Postgres Extension for AI"
Core architectural claim: PostgreSQL + RuVector is a heavyweight, tiered client-server model and 'cognitive database' serving as the ultimate Trojan horse for Rust-powered AI in standard SQL environments.
PostgreSQL-first LifeOS role: Acts as the global, long-term semantic memory, global knowledge base, and heavy relational analytics engine (the 'Global Brain').
Canonical state: Stored in PostgreSQL + RuVector as the editable canonical user-space and codebase, including massive vector indices and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs).
Temporary/derived state: Flat files/directories are derived materializations (projections) generated by envctl; redb handles transient 'scratchpad' state.
Data/entities: Relational data, JSON metadata, AI vector embeddings, code blocks as structured ASTs, and causal graphs.
Identity keys/schema: 230+ native SQL functions; keys include BLAKE3_Hash for deduplication and cryptographic witness chains using SHAKE256.
Reads: Hybrid search combining traditional relational filtering with advanced AI vector/graph search in a single SQL query.
Writes: High-frequency writes buffered in redb (microseconds) then flushed asynchronously to PostgreSQL (milliseconds).
Ingestion role: Nushell + nu_plugin_ruvector parses files into ASTs and streams structured data into redb/PostgreSQL.
Database edit/branch role: COW (Copy-On-Write) branching in .rvf and PostgreSQL allows isolated semantic refactoring and simulations.
Materialization role: envctl projects database rows back into flat files for Nix/Cargo build pipelines.
PostgreSQL relation: Primary relational and semantic storage host; replaces flat-file source control with a database-hosted codebase.
RuVector relation: Native Rust extension providing HNSW indexing, GNN capabilities, and 2-4 bit KV-cache quantization.
redb relation: Local AI scratchpad, high-speed write buffer, and zero-overhead embedding/inference deduplication cache.
AgentDB relation: Serverless .rvf 'Cognitive Container' for portable agent memory, holding MicroLoRAs and self-learning loops.
Nushell/nu_plugin relation: Transforms terminal symbols into database structures; native plugin (nu_plugin_ruvector) avoids JSON serialization bottlenecks.
envctl relation: The 'Materializer' and environment controller that manages import/export and syncing between redb and PostgreSQL.
LifeOS/UDS relation: Front-door Mission Control UI communicating via Unix Domain Sockets (UDS) to read shared redb/Postgres state.
Nix/Yazelix relation: Yazelix is the workspace engine; Nix provides deterministic static musl builds to bypass the /nix/store dynamic linking trap.
Dependencies/order: Nushell -> redb -> Postgres -> RuVector -> Materialization (envctl) -> Nix Build.
Task-graph phase: Includes Ensemble Simulation (ATAS) and Future Prediction (RuvLTRA) before committing code changes.
Acceptence evidence: Sub-millisecond queries, 52,000+ inserts/sec, and deterministic isolation of hallucinations via MinCut.
Conflicts: GitHub repository appears 'dirty' due to auto-generated napi-rs/wasm-pack bindings; pristine code is on Crates.io.
Missing detail: Specific implementation of 'FlexNetOS/nu_plugin' beyond redb/Postgres bridge is not fully detailed.
Exact terminology: Temporal Strange Attractor, Echo-State Network, Proportional Intelligence, Semantic IDE, Cognitive Container, Witness Chains.
